summary: R2 systems_architect read-only review accepts the fixes for the two prior REQUEST_CHANGES findings. Regency outcomes now semantically validate that regentCharacterId references an existing, alive, non-incapacitated M3 character, including malformed save rejection after checksum/hash restamping. One-candidate low-support succession resolution now returns a structured succession-state-invalid rejection before commit and preserves the pending crisis/world hash instead of generating an invalid disputed self-rival outcome. No new architecture, dependency, UI, Electron, Pixi, Node IO, random, real-time, manual battle, telemetry, server, or multiplayer scope was introduced.

tests_run.result: On feat/m6-succession-completion. Before writing this handoff, current status showed only the six expected modified source/test files.
Focused R2 regressions passed: 1 file passed; 2 tests passed, 15 skipped.
Focused protocol/succession suite passed: 2 files passed, 27 tests passed.
24 test files passed, 186 tests passed. Node emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, M4 232f62ab, M5 bf042e42.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture:check passed.
Architecture boundaries passed; sim determinism passed; root Vitest 37 files / 287 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file / 4 tests passed.

tests_run.command: rg -n "Math\.random|Date\.now|performance\.now|node:fs|node:path|\bReact\b|\bPixi\b|\bElectron\b|eval\(|new Function|manual node|\btelemetry\b|\bserver\b|\bmultiplayer\b" packages\protocol\src\index.ts packages\save-format\src\index.ts packages\sim-core\src\command-query-v1.ts packages\sim-core\src\world-state-v0.ts tests\protocol-command-query-schema.test.ts tests\sim-core-m3-succession-state-machine.test.ts
tests_run.result: No forbidden coupling, random/time API, arbitrary-code execution, manual node battle, telemetry, server, or multiplayer hits in reviewed source/test files.

decisions: ACCEPT: Prior finding 1 is fixed. packages/sim-core/src/world-state-v0.ts now passes characterIds and availableCharacterIds into validateM3SuccessionOutcomeReferences and validates regency regentCharacterId through validateM3SuccessionRegentReference. Missing regents produce bad-reference; dead or incapacitated regents produce invalid-schema.

ACCEPT: Prior finding 1 has adequate regression coverage. tests/sim-core-m3-succession-state-machine.test.ts now covers unavailable regent invariant rejection and malformed save rejection for a missing regent after restamping the mutated save snapshot hash and envelope checksum.

ACCEPT: Prior finding 2 is fixed. packages/sim-core/src/command-query-v1.ts now makes resolveM3SuccessionOutcome return an explicit success/error result and rejects low-support one-candidate disputed resolution before commit with succession-state-invalid.
ACCEPT: Prior finding 2 has adequate regression coverage. The one-candidate low-support test asserts structured rejection, unchanged world hash, and the crisis remaining pending with null outcome.

ACCEPT: Architecture boundaries remain intact: sim-core stays decoupled from React/Pixi/Electron/DOM/Node IO/real time, authority remains through GameCommand paths, and M6 legitimacy integration reuses the accepted source/profile substrate instead of introducing a parallel system.

ACCEPT: Determinism and stable ordering are preserved. Candidate ordering still uses supportTotalBps with characterId tie-breakers; no Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, host IO, or object-graph serialization was introduced.
ACCEPT: Save/protocol/schema behavior is explicit and tested for round-trip, malformed/future trigger rejection, malformed regent semantic rejection, and event-tail succession preservation.
risks: Legitimacy magnitudes for succession outcomes remain Alpha constants and may need later balance tuning, but they are deterministic and source-coded.
Current review accepts the R2 implementation diff only; downstream M6 start-to-victory tasks still need to prove the full recognized-order victory path using these surfaces.
requested_action: Lead may treat M6-SUCCESSION-COMPLETION-001 systems review R2 as ACCEPT and continue orchestration toward the next required review or closure step.
